title: Rich Pillowy Egyptian Shortening Bisquettes
description: Moist, aromatic, and delightfully crumbly Egyptian treats made with shortening, perfect for midnight cravings. These bisquettes are free of onion and garlic for a pleasant flavor without any overpowering taste.
instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or a silicone mat.
Whisk together flour, sugar, bakingsoda, and salt in a medium bowl.
Add butter shortening and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Beat in eggs until the dough just comes together, being careful not to overmix.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead a few times until it becomes pliable.
Use a cookie scoop or your hands to shape the dough into small balls, about 1 inch in diameter.
Place the balls onto the prepared baking sheet, leaving about 1 inch of space between each bisquette.
Dip the top of each bisquette into the orange blossom water and sprinkle with fleishmanns instant vanilla.
Bake for 12-15 minutes, or until lightly golden brown on the bottom.
Allow the bisquettes to cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely,
Serve warm or at room temperature.
